items.correct: The 'blowing out' of the fires of craving, hatred and delusion — liberation from the cycle of rebirth (samsara), not a heavenly afterlife.
items.misconception: Nirvana is the Buddhist version of heaven.
items.random_wrong: Nirvana is the name of a Buddhist god.
items.why: Students map nirvana onto Christian heaven — a place you go after dying. Nirvana is the cessation of the conditions that cause rebirth; it is more an end than a destination.

items.q: What are the main Christian theories of atonement?
items.correct: Penal substitution (Christ bore the punishment for sin), Christus Victor (Christ defeated evil powers through the cross), ransom theory (Christ paid a price to free humanity), and moral exemplar (Christ's death inspires love). Different traditions emphasise different theories.
items.misconception: All Christians believe Jesus died only to pay God back for sin.
items.random_wrong: Atonement means Jesus apologised to humans for being born divine.
items.why: Penal substitution dominates Protestant evangelicalism but isn't the only or oldest model. Christus Victor was prevalent in the early church and Orthodox traditions still emphasise it. Students often treat one model as 'the' Christian view of atonement.

items.q: What is the doctrine of the Trinity?
items.correct: God is one being existing in three persons (Father, Son, Holy Spirit) — distinct in person but united in essence; Christians remain monotheistic.
items.misconception: Christians believe in three different gods.
items.random_wrong: The Trinity refers to heaven, earth and hell.
items.why: Students treat 'three persons' as 'three gods'. The orthodox position is one God in three distinct persons sharing one substance — a notoriously difficult concept students often misrepresent as polytheism.
The answer examiners want is: Restoring a broken relationship. “Punishing wrongdoers” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which is NOT typically a cause of war?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Forgiveness. “Greed” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In which Beatitude does Jesus bless 'the peacemakers' (Matthew 5)?
items.why: The real figure is 9. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
